description: 1861–1865 civil war in the United States between the North and the South over the enslavement of African-Americans
description_wiki: The American Civil War (April 12, 1861 – May 26, 1865; also known by other names) was a civil war in the United States between the Union ("the North") and the Confederacy ("the South"), which was formed in 1861 by states that had seceded from the Union to preserve slavery in the United States, which they saw as threatened because of the election of Abraham Lincoln and the growing abolitionist movement in the North.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki:
Florida (  FLORR-ih-də; Spanish: [floˈɾiða] ) is a state in the Southeastern and South Atlantic
regions of the United States.

description: consolidated city-county and county seat of Clarke County, Georgia, United States
description_wiki: Athens is a consolidated city-county in the U.S. state of Georgia.

description: head of state of the German Empire (1871–1918)
description_wiki: The German Emperor (German: Deutscher Kaiser, pronounced [ˈdɔʏtʃɐ ˈkaɪzɐ] ) was the official title of the head of state and hereditary ruler of the German Empire.

description: capital city of the German federated state of Lower Saxony
description_wiki: Hanover (German: Hannover [haˈnoːfɐ] ) is the capital and largest city of the German state of Lower Saxony.

description: sovereign state in South America
description_wiki: Bolivia, officially the Plurinational State of Bolivia, is a landlocked country located in central South America.

description: largest city in the U.S. state of South Carolina
description_wiki: Charleston is the most populous city in the U.S. state of South Carolina.

description: sailing ship carrying English and Dutch Pilgrims from England to North America
description_wiki: Mayflower was an English square-rigged merchant sailing ship, active from before 1609 until 1622.

description: American physician and judge, signatory of the Declaration of Independence (1729–1795)
description_wiki: Josiah Bartlett (December 2, 1729 [O.S. November 21, 1729] – May 19, 1795) was an American Founding Father, physician, statesman, a delegate to the Continental Congress for New Hampshire, and a signatory to the Declaration of Independence and Articles of Confederation.

description: 1776 assertion of colonial America's independence from Great Britain
description_wiki: The Declaration of Independence, formally The unanimous Declaration of the thirteen united States of America in the original printing, is the founding document of the United States.

description: capital city of Georgia, United States
description_wiki: Atlanta is the capital and most populous city of the U.S. state of Georgia.

description: performing arts college at Roosevelt University, Chicago, Illinois
description_wiki: The Chicago College of Performing Arts is the performing arts college of Roosevelt University in Chicago, Illinois, United States.

description: capital and largest city of Massachusetts, United States
description_wiki: Boston () is the capital and most populous city of the U.S. state of Massachusetts.

description: Polish musician (1876-1957)
description_wiki: Josef Casimir Hofmann (originally Józef Kazimierz Hofmann; January 20, 1876 – February 16, 1957) was a Polish-American pianist, composer, music teacher, and inventor.

description: American actor and painter (1829–1905)
description_wiki: Joseph Jefferson III (February 20, 1829 – April 23, 1905), often known as Joe Jefferson, was an American actor.

description: English actor-manager (1857-1907)
description_wiki: Richard Mansfield (24 May 1857 – 30 August 1907) was a German-born English actor-manager best known for his performances in Shakespeare plays, Gilbert and Sullivan operas, and the play Dr.

description: protagonist of a classic German legend
description_wiki: Faust ( FOWST; German: [faʊst] ) is the protagonist of a classic German legend based on the historical Johann Georg Faust (c. 1480–1540).

description: American actress and businesswoman (1868–1940)
description_wiki: Maxine Elliott (February 5, 1868 – March 5, 1940) also known as Little Jessie, Dettie or by her birth name Jessie Dermott, was an American actress and businesswoman.

description: German soprano (1888–1976)
description_wiki: Charlotte "Lotte" Pauline Sophie Lehmann (February 27, 1888 – August 26, 1976) was a German-American dramatic soprano noted for her successful performances with international opera houses, on the recital stage and in teaching.

description: city in Duval County, and largest city in State of Florida, United States
description_wiki: Jacksonville (US:  , JAK-sən-vil; colloquially nicknamed Jax) is the most populous city proper in the U.S. state of Florida, located on the Atlantic coast of northeastern Florida.

description: county seat of Seminole County, Florida, United States
description_wiki: Sanford is a city in and the county seat of Seminole County, Florida, United States.

description: county seat of Palm Beach County, Florida, United States
description_wiki: West Palm Beach is a city in and the county seat of Palm Beach County, Florida, United States.

description: daily newspaper in Jacksonville, Florida
holiday celebrated in the United States on the fourth Thursday in November
description_wiki: Thanksgiving is a federal holiday in the United States celebrated on the fourth Thursday of November (which became the uniform date country-wide in 1941).

aliases: Federal Music Project of the WPA
description: formed in 1935 under Federal Project No. One of the Works Progress Administration to employ, train, and rehabilitate unemployed musicians
description_wiki: The Federal Music Project (FMP) was a part of the New Deal program Federal Project Number One provided by the U.S. federal government which employed musicians, conductors and composers during the Great Depression.

description: USA theatre company 1935 - 1939
description_wiki: The Federal Theatre Project (FTP; 1935–1939) was a theatre program established during the Great Depression as part of the New Deal to fund live artistic performances and entertainment programs in the United States.

description: president of the United States from 1933 to 1945 (1882–1945)
description_wiki: Franklin Delano Roosevelt (January 30, 1882 – April 12, 1945), also known as FDR, was the 32nd president of the United States, serving from 1933 until his death in 1945.
